response.justification: The candidate is a versatile senior full-stack engineer with genuine strengths in testing, developer tooling, and mentoring, but he does not meet the core minimum qualifications for this role. His professional C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments plus a thesis project, far short of the required 8 years. His large-scale infrastructure exposure is limited to about two years of low-latency DLang microservices work, and his recent career centers on TypeScript/Node.js product engineering rather than cloud compute platforms. He also lacks a formal technical-leadership track record of owning cross-team strategy, priorities, and deliverables.
strengths: Hands-on low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (microservices handling millions of requests per second)
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, authored test frameworks, and broad polyglot experience
Proven mentoring and knowledge-sharing record through workshops, courses, and conference talks
concerns: Roughly 1 year of professional C++ versus the required 8 years
Distributed systems and infrastructure experience (~2 years) falls well below the 5-year requirement, with no cloud compute platform background
No formal technical-lead role driving cross-team alignment, priorities, and technical strategy
